# pdf2md

> Convert PDFs to clean structured Markdown in ~10 milliseconds, built for AI
> agents and LLM applications. Reading a PDF as Markdown costs ~10× fewer
> tokens than reading its pages as images, and preserves headings, lists,
> tables, and multi-column reading order. Single native binary (Rust + pdfium):
> no Python, no ML models, no network, no cost. MIT, always free. Ships as a
> CLI, a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server (`pdf2md --mcp`, warm engine,
> low-millisecond per document), and a Claude Code agent skill
> (`scripts/install-skill.sh`).

Born-digital PDFs only (reads the text layer; no OCR — use a vision model for
scanned documents). Benchmarked and CI-gated against pymupdf4llm, markitdown,
and pdftotext: first on judged quality, hand-labelled tables, noise, and text
fidelity, and on external ground truth authored by the Tabula project.

## Docs

- [Agent guide](docs/agents.md): why agents should convert PDFs to Markdown,
  quick start for the Claude Code skill / MCP server / CLI, speed and quality
  comparisons, efficiency tips, FAQ.
- [Benchmarks & methodology](docs/benchmarks.md): the five benchmark suites,
  corpora, CI gates, and how to reproduce every number.
- [README](README.md): install and basic usage.
- [Skill definition](skills/pdf2md/SKILL.md): the agent-skill instructions.

## Usage

- CLI: `pdf2md file.pdf` prints Markdown to stdout; `pdf2md a.pdf b.pdf`
  batches with one warm engine; `--stats` reports tokens saved.
- MCP: `pdf2md --mcp` serves the `convert_pdf{path}` tool over stdio.
- Skill: `scripts/install-skill.sh` installs a self-contained skill into
  `~/.claude/skills/pdf2md/`.
